title: Report on Outstanding Species at Risk Last update July 2008 - Canada.ca
content:
## Report on Outstanding Species at Risk Last update July 2008
Under the Species at Risk Act (SARA), the Governor in Council can, within nine months of receiving an assessment of the status of a species by the Committee on the Status of Endangered Wildlife in Canada (COSEWIC), either;
- accept the assessment and add the species to Schedule 1;
- decide to not add the species to Schedule 1; or
- refer the assessment back to COSEWIC for further information or consideration.
The current status of species under extended consultations or for which a Not List decision was made by the GiC to accommodate further consultations. Decisions to Refer species back to COSEWIC for further consideration are also included.
- Batch 1 - COSEWIC Assessments: May 2002, November 2002, May 2003
- Batch 2 - COSEWIC Assessments: November 2003, May 2004
- Batch 3 - COSEWIC Assessments: November 2004, May 2005
- Batch 4 - COSEWIC Assessments: April 2006
